episodes.title: The Lownie Report: The Campaign to Discredit Epstein's Victims
episodes.description:
An incredible episode of The Lownie Report this week with Anne Cheever, who has done some brilliant work over the past few years investigating the network of people surrounding the Yorks and Ghislaine Maxwell who have used a multitude of techniques to try and discredit the brave victims who have come forward and spoken publicly about their abuse. 

Anne has called this network the ‘She-Wolves’ and I highly recommend her series on Substack which looks at how figures like Fergie, Victoria Hervey and George Bing Tonks have played a significant role in muddying the waters, for example with the ridiculous notion that the now infamous photo of the late Virginia Giuffre was fake or that the victims were not telling the truth.

We discussed parallels with the Amber Heard trial, how bots are used en-masse across social media to spread misinformation - I have experienced the latter myself - and how Dropbox has been used as a repository for files that help make this campaign of hate possible and highly organised. 

We also discussed Maria Farmer, one of Epstein's earliest known victims and a woman of extraordinary courage, who has had to endure not only the trauma of her abuse but the very deliberate infiltration of her inner circle by bad actors secretly recording her private conversations in order to sabotage her legal case against the FBI. 

Anne also makes a compelling case that the ultimate goal of this whole network is to secure a presidential pardon for Ghislaine Maxwell, who is already receiving what can only be described as extraordinarily preferential treatment in prison; private computer access, her mail handled personally by the warden, conditions that frankly sound less like a federal penitentiary and more like a rather inconvenient hotel. The message being sent could not be clearer and it is a deeply troubling one for anyone who cares about justice for the survivors.

Both Anne and I have come up against disgusting behaviour and allegations since trying to shine a light on the Epstein saga and I salute her tenacity and bravery in carrying on the quest for truth despite such powerful opposition and abuse. I can’t recommend her Substack enough and I hope you will enjoy this insightful interview that is as riveting as it is horrifying. 

00:00:00 Introduction
00:00:11 Anne Cheever on her Epstein research
00:01:31 How Epstein survivors have been silenced
00:02:37 Virginia Giuffre and the online campaign
00:06:22 Who are the “She Wolves”?
00:08:37 Lady Victoria Hervey and possible motivations
00:10:26 Protecting the Epstein “file class”
00:12:21 Democrats, Republicans and the Epstein cover-up
00:12:57 Victims, lawyers and control
00:15:06 The psychological toll on survivors
00:16:46 Virginia Giuffre’s final year
00:18:11 George B Tonks enters the story
00:21:35 Secret recordings, smears and social media
00:23:37 Why does the media platform these claims?
00:25:07 Social media, cease and desist letters and accountability
00:25:48 Lady Victoria Hervey’s links to Andrew and Sarah Ferguson
00:27:31 Independent journalists investigating the story
00:29:20 Did Hervey present herself as an Epstein victim?
00:29:53 Jessica Krauss, Amber Heard and online smear campaigns
00:32:39 Ghislaine Maxwell, Jay Beecher and the pardon campaign
00:34:02 Bots, influencers and manufactured narratives
00:35:08 Bots versus real people online
00:39:55 The personal cost of investigating Epstein
00:41:37 Antonia Marshall, Sarah Ferguson and the wider network
00:43:00 How the online smear ecosystem developed
00:45:12 The MAGA connection
00:48:10 Why isn’t the mainstream press doing more?
00:50:03 Is there now a fightback?
00:51:31 Melania Trump and renewed Epstein questions
00:52:53 Will more Epstein material be released?
00:54:25 Will more victims come forward?
00:55:54 Closing thoughts

episodes.title: The Lownie Report Podcast: Mixed Messages from Police Investigating Andrew & When I Met Fergie to Discuss 'Entitled'
episodes.description: We discuss the latest developments regarding Andrew and Fergie and also cover some pertinent parallels taking us back, via Mountbatten, to the Cambridge Spy Ring and how I first learnt about the British establishment’s cover-up playbook while writing Stalin’s Englishman: The Lives of Guy Burgess . The Lownie Report is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. We cover a fascinating 1997 TV interview with Sarah Ferguson that has recently been uploaded to YouTube by Ruby Wax. It was recorded when some of her biggest early scandals were only just in the rear-view mirror. Wax’s inimitable interview technique is highly effective in revealing as much about Ferguson’s modus operandi post-scandal as the multitude of reasons she gives for her mistakes. She claims ignorance and naivety in her consistent attraction to the wrong men and bad decisions, refers to the solace of the words of St Francis of Assisi, compares herself to Morgan Freeman’s character in Shawshank Redemption and doesn’t demur when Wax generously posits that she’s actually more of a Robin Hood figure, since she donated some of her £5m Coutts overdraft to charity. As I wrote in Entitled , the former Duchess would wheel out exactly the same excuses after the scandals that peppered the following three decades of her career, ostensibly outside the Royal Family but very much operating within its protection via her ex-husband. I also discuss my meeting with Sarah Ferguson when I began work on my book back in 2022. She had all the charisma you might expect and brought her recent Mills & Boon book and some Ginger & Moss products as a gift, and initially seemed co-operative. Discussions with her team carried on for a few weeks before interviewees began to drop out and letters from lawyers began to drop in through the letterbox. The Lownie Report is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. The conversation also looks at the mixed messages coming out of the police investigation, with briefing that seems contradictory at times, and how there seems a distinct possibility that if no charges are brought, the public will be told the matter is closed as far as the Crown and the government are concerned. We also looked at what effect this has actually had on the Royal Family and whether anything substantial has changed in respect of public accountability and transparency. I quoted Harold Macmillan’s line: “When my gamekeeper shoots a fox, he doesn’t go and hang it up outside the Master of Foxhounds’ drawing room; he buries it out of sight.” An apt metaphor and an attitude that still seemed apparent in the way the establishment covered up for Andrew until Virginia Giuffre and the Epstein Files forced them into action. An example of the “Jolly Good Chaps” theory of history, which has a habit of protecting men who are quite the opposite. The Lownie Report is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. The updated paperback of Entitled is out now and is this week’s Number One Sunday Times Bestseller! Order your copy here with 50% off! To hear more, visit andrewlownie.substack.com
